---
title: Tiered
description: L1 (memory) + L2 (Redis) with promotion and write-through.
icon: Layers
---

## What is tiered caching?

The tiered adapter combines a fast **L1 in-memory** layer with a durable **L2 remote** layer (Redis, Upstash, etc.). On a read, it checks L1 first. If the entry is missing, it reads L2 and promotes the result into L1 for subsequent requests.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache ioredis
```

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { memoryAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/memory";
import { redisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";
import { tieredAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/tiered";
import Redis from "ioredis";

const redisClient = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!);

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: tieredAdapter({
    l1: memoryAdapter(),
    l2: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  }),
});
```

## Options

```ts
tieredAdapter({
  l1: CacheAdapter;  // Fast local layer (required)
  l2: CacheAdapter;  // Durable remote layer (required)
})
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `l1` | L1 adapter (fast, e.g. memory) |
| `l2` | L2 adapter (durable, e.g. Redis or Upstash) |

L2 hits are always promoted to L1 and writes always go to both layers.

## Read path

1. Check L1 — return if found and not expired.
2. Check L2 — if found, promote to L1 and return.
3. Return `null` on miss.

## Write path

Every `set` writes to both L1 and L2 simultaneously.
